title: Confusion with profound dehydration

chart.oneLiner: 70F with type 2 diabetes presents with several days of progressive confusion, polyuria, and profound dehydration.

opening: So thirsty… I can't think straight.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 70-year-old woman, lethargic, very dry, confused. Poor historian.

COLLATERAL (provide ONLY if the student asks family/chart/medication list): Over the past several days she's had increasing thirst, frequent urination, and progressive confusion. Reduced intake the last day. Had a cough/cold recently (possible infectious precipitant). Type 2 diabetes, sometimes misses her metformin. No insulin. MEDS: metformin, lisinopril, hydrochlorothiazide.

HPI (from patient — limited): very thirsty, confused, weak.
PMH: type 2 diabetes, hypertension. SOCIAL: lives alone.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: lethargic, profoundly dehydrated (dry mucous membranes, poor skin turgor, sunken eyes).
- Vitals: BP 96/60, HR 116, RR 20, 37.9°C, SpO2 97% RA.
- Neuro: confused/lethargic, no focal deficit, no neck stiffness.
- NO fruity/ketotic breath odor (helps distinguish from DKA). Cardiac: tachycardic.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis.
- The patient is confused: vague answers. Provide COLLATERAL (polyuria/polydipsia, missed metformin, recent cold) only when explicitly asked family/chart/what happened.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.SerumOsm: Calculated/measured serum osmolality >330 mOsm/kg — markedly elevated.
labs.Ketones: Serum beta-hydroxybutyrate minimal; urine ketones trace (NOT the heavy ketosis of DKA).
labs.VBG: pH 7.34, HCO3 20 — only mild acidosis (no severe anion-gap acidosis).
labs.BMP: Na 148 (corrected higher), K 4.8, BUN 48, Cr 2.0 (prerenal), Glu 880.
labs.Cultures: Sepsis screen for precipitant — UA, CXR, cultures.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Irregular rhythm with at least three distinct P-wave morphologies and varying PP/PR/RR intervals; rate ~110.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Irregular rhythm with at least three distinct P-wave morphologies and varying PP/PR/RR intervals; rate ~110. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray
cxr.findings: Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.
cxr.verify: Pick a CC normal PA chest film.
cxr.imageDescription: Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.
searchTerms: chest radiograph Screen pneumonia precipitant; here

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your assessment, list your differential (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (leading,7). Diabetic ketoacidosis (core,4). Hypoglycemia (CANT-MISS to exclude,3). Sepsis (CANT-MISS,3). Stroke (CANT-MISS,3). Other metabolic/electrolyte (core,3). Named leading dx w/ justification — marked hyperglycemia, dehydration, minimal ketosis (core,2).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Hypoglycemia; Sepsis; Stroke. Also reasonable: Diabetic ketoacidosis; Other metabolic/electrolyte.

coreActions.item: Named leading dx w/ justification — marked hyperglycemia, dehydration, minimal ketosis

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial diagnostic workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: Glucose (CANT-MISS,3). BMP/electrolytes incl. potassium (CANT-MISS,3). Serum osmolality (3). Serum/urine ketones + VBG to distinguish from DKA (3). Search for precipitant — UA, CXR, cultures (2). EKG (1). PENALTY: not checking potassium before planning insulin (-2).

penalties.item: not checking potassium before planning insulin

contraindicatedTests: not checking potassium before planning insulin
orderSensitiveActions: not checking potassium before planning insulin

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = sinus tachycardia; assess for potassium effects; no acute ischemia. CREDIT: rate & rhythm (6); comments on looking for hyper/hypokalemic changes (4); no acute ischemia (3); systematic read (2). PENALTY: over-calling ischemia (-3).
keyFindings: irregular rhythm with varying PR intervals
criticalFindings: Multifocal atrial tachycardia (≥3 P-wave morphologies, rate >100).
commonMistakes: calling it atrial fibrillation because the rhythm is irregular
missing the discrete varying P waves
steps.idealAnswer: Multifocal atrial tachycardia: ≥3 distinct P-wave morphologies with varying PR intervals, irregular, rate >100.

criticalActions: Multifocal atrial tachycardia (≥3 P-wave morphologies, rate >100).
coreActions: irregular rhythm with varying PR intervals

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = clear film. CREDIT: identifies a normal film (6); notes it screens for a precipitating pneumonia (4). PENALTY: inventing pathology (-2).
keyFindings: notes it screens for a precipitating pneumonia
criticalFindings: Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.
steps.idealAnswer: Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.

coreActions.item: notes it screens for a precipitating pneumonia

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Given glucose 880, osm >330, and minimal ketones, what is your diagnosis and how does it differ from DKA?
steps.rubric: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (6). Distinguishes from DKA — much higher glucose/osmolality, minimal ketosis, only mild acidosis (4).

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH DKA/HHS: aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, LR/NS (CANT-MISS,7); potassium repletion/monitoring – hold insulin if K<3.3 (CANT-MISS,5); IV insulin infusion after K safe (5); add dextrose when glucose <300 (3); identify and treat precipitant (3); frequent BMP/glucose monitoring (2). PENALTY: starting insulin before checking/repleting K (-4); insulin without fluids (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: HHS: prioritize ABCs and volume status, then potassium, then glucose. Aggressive IV fluids (LR 15–20 mL/kg/h initially; ~average 8–10 L deficit), then ½NS/LR based on corrected sodium; add D5 when glucose <300. Replete and monitor potassium (give K if <5.3, hold insulin if <3.3). IV insulin infusion after K is safe. Find and treat the precipitant. Frequent BMP/glucose.

coreActions.item: IV insulin infusion after K is safe

bonusActions.item: Monitor for euglycemic DKA if on SGLT2i

optionalActions: IV insulin infusion after K is safe
Monitor for euglycemic DKA if on SGLT2i

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 70F with type 2 diabetes presents with several days of progressive confusion, polyuria, and profound dehydration. The diagnosis is Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Neuro: confused/lethargic, no focal deficit, no neck stiffness.
Neuro: confused/lethargic, no focal deficit, no neck stiffness.

Neuro: confused/lethargic, no focal deficit, no neck stiffness.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.

Screen for pneumonia as a precipitant; here essentially clear.

Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.
